about:
I am a PhD student at the Chinese Information Processing Laboratory, Institute of Software, Chinese Academy of Sciences (ISCAS), where I am advised by Prof. Xianpei Han.

My research centers on the alignment of large language models — how we can keep increasingly capable models reliably aligned with human intent, at scale. My work spans safety systems, automated alignment, and scalable oversight.

select_publications.title: The Meta-Agent Challenge: Are Current Agents Capable of Autonomous Agent Development?
select_publications.summary: A benchmark testing whether agents can autonomously build other agents — and where they still fall short.

select_publications.title: P²O: Joint Policy and Prompt Optimization
select_publications.summary: Alternates RL policy updates with discrete prompt evolution to escape advantage collapse on hard samples, then internalizes the gains back into the model.

select_publications.title: AutoAlign: Get Your LLM Aligned with Minimal Annotations
select_publications.summary: An automated pipeline that aligns an LLM with little to no human annotation.

select_publications.title: Transferable Post-training via Inverse Value Learning
select_publications.summary: Models post-training as a logit-level value residual learned by a separate network, transferable across base models of different sizes.

select_publications.title: SoFA: Shielded On-the-fly Alignment via Priority Rule Following
select_publications.summary: Keeps models safe at inference time by following prioritized rules that can shift on the fly.
